title: Spreadsheets, Python, Reproducibility, and Calculation Checks · Mastery Check
summary: Turn a hand calculation into a reusable, tested worksheet and small Python model. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a reproducible calculation tool with test cases. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use relative and absolute cell references intentionally in copied formulas.
Add input constraints, units, labels, and audit cells to a spreadsheet model.
Express a unit-aware calculation as readable Python variables, functions, and output.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 22 units, changes by −9 units, then by +8 units. Calculate the final quantity. Use the result to decide Reproducible Report for a reproducible calculation tool with test cases.
steps.why: The Reproducible Report result for a reproducible calculation tool with test cases is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Publish inputs, method, version, results, limitations, and rerun instructions.
